h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: `C4` goes under “address,” because that is the column and row spot. I need to check the number sitting in that cell for “value”... I almost want to write `C4` again, but that would be the place, not the number.

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner consistently practices placing bar lines based on the time signature and verifying understanding of beat units.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Bottom 8 names the eighth note as the one-beat unit. Top 3 counts three eighth-note beats in the bar, so the bar line goes here. Speed would need a separate tempo mark, correct?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: Learner applies the beat-unit rule to a new bottom number, correctly places the bar line, and independently raises the tempo distinction.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The cards check that: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Tempo sits outside those labels. Write 3/8; keep the bar line after the third eighth note.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: Tutor responds to the learner's persistent speed question by physically adding a tempo card and stating the separation rule, changing the representation.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The cards check that: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Tempo sits outside those labels. Write 3/8; keep the bar line after the third eighth note.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The physical separation of the tempo card is a concrete, reusable scaffold that makes the abstract distinction visible and directly answers the learner's confusion.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists; no revised framing to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will place all four force arrow tiles correctly using the rule that the tile names the source and the arrowhead gives direction.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Friction goes there: the surface’s force on the cart is leftward. Push goes only where the applied force points on the cart, so if the hand pushes right, the push tile goes on the right arrow.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly places the friction tile and explains push, enacting the tutor's rule without further prompting.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates sustained pressure; the exchange proceeds smoothly without tutor strategy change.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no contingent adaptation.
